Catherine de' Medici
1519 – 1589
Catherine de' Medici
The Italian Orphan Who Ruled France  ·  1519 – 1589
Press ▶ Play · Arrow keys to navigate ·
The Renaissance · 14th – 17th Century
The Renaissance was a period of cultural, artistic, political, and economic rebirth in Europe. It also marked the birth of one of history's most influential women.
🌍 The Renaissance began in Italy in the 14th century
Florence · 1519
Born April 13, 1519 into the wealthy and powerful Medici family, Catherine de' Medici was orphaned within just 15 days of her birth. She was two weeks old. And the most important orphan in Europe.
😢 Sole heir to the entire Medici fortune
Florence · 1519–1533
She grew up in the heart of the Renaissance. She was tutored in Latin, Greek, art and even astronomy.
🎨 Florence was the cultural capital of the world
Vatican · 1533
Her uncle was Pope Clement VII. He arranged her marriage to the Prince of France. She was only 14.
⛪ The Pope personally officiated the wedding
Paris · 1547
Her husband became King Henry II. She was Queen of France — but he was in love with his mistress. Despite this, she gave birth to 10 children. Three of which became future Kings of France.
👸 Three of her children became Kings of France
Paris · 1547–1559
Henry was a proud warrior king. He wore his mistress's colors at every jousting tournament. Catherine wore black. Always black.
⚔️ Henry expanded France but kept Catherine in the background
Paris · June 1559
At a jousting tournament, a lance splintered into Henry's eye. He died 10 days later. Catherine was 40. And suddenly in charge.
🏇 France banned jousting immediately after
France · 1559–1589
Three sons. Three kings. She ruled behind all of them for 30 years. Regent, advisor, and the real power in France.
👩‍⚖️ One of the longest periods of female power in French history
Paris · Legacy
She built the Tuileries Palace. She brought ballet to France. She introduced new foods, fashion, and perfume to the French court.
🎨 Italian Renaissance culture transformed France forever
Paris · August 1572
She was also known to many as the "Serpent Queen." During the St. Bartholomew's Day Massacre, thousands of Protestants were killed. Her exact role is still debated.
⚖️ Historians still disagree on Catherine's responsibility
Blois · January 5th, 1589
Catherine died at the age of 69 on January 5th, 1589. From orphan to Queen Mother of France. She held a kingdom together for 30 years. Not bad.
Catherine de' Medici · 1519–1589
End of Presentation
